name: Chocolate Macaroons
recipeInstructions:
1. Freeze the almonds for 30 minutes.
2. Preheat the oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit and line two baking sheets with baking parchment.
3. In a food processor, combine the almonds and confectioners sugar and pulse on and off for 20 to 30 seconds, or until powdery. You should be able to rub the mixture between your fingers and feel almost no grit.
4. Add the cocoa powder and pulse very briefly just to mix.
5. In a large mixing bowl, beat the egg whites to soft peaks and beat in the granulated sugar until glossy (1 minute).
6. Carefully fold the almond-cocoa mixture into the egg white mixture, being careful not to crush too much air out of the egg whites.
7. Using a piping bag fitted with a 1/2-inch plain tip, pipe 1-1/2 inch circles of the mixture onto the parchment-lined baking sheets.
8. Gently place a whole almond in the center of each macaroon.
9. Bake in the oven for 25 to 30 minutes, or until firm, turning the pans around halfway through the cooking time to ensure even cooking.
10. Transfer the sheets of paper onto damp tea towels and let stand for 5 minutes before peeling the macaroons off and finishing cooling them on a rack.
11. Serve at room temperature or store in an airtight tin, layered with waxed paper, for up to 2 weeks.
